qa_pairs.question: How can I load the same set of default modules that are available on other national clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: You should load the `StdEnv/2023` module by running `$ module load StdEnv/2023`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the default compiler loaded by the `StdEnv/2023` module?
qa_pairs.answer: The `StdEnv/2023` module will load the gcc compiler as the default.

qa_pairs.question: How do I compile GPU code with CUDA on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: To compile GPU code with CUDA, you need to load the `cuda` module in addition to `StdEnv/2023`. For example: `$ module load StdEnv/2023 cuda/12.6`.

qa_pairs.question: On which Trillium nodes is the `cuda` module available?
qa_pairs.answer: The `cuda` module is not available on Trillium CPU nodes; it only works on the GPU login node and GPU compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: Which types of codes require recompilation on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Codes that weren't using the CVMFS software stack or that used absolute paths will have to be recompiled. It is highly recommended to recompile all your code, even if it did use the CVMFS stack on Niagara.

qa_pairs.question: Will my existing virtual Python environments work on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: No, your virtual Python environments will not work and must be recreated due to changes in the users' directory.

qa_pairs.question: Is the Anaconda module available on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: No, there is no Anaconda module available on Trillium. Users should switch to using virtual environments instead.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find a quickstart guide for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: A new quickstart guide is available at [[Trillium Quickstart]].

qa_pairs.question: What is the support email address for Trillium?
Is there a self-guided course available for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there is a self-guided course titled "Intro to SciNet and Trillium" available at https://education.scinet.utoronto.ca/course/view.php?id=1389.
